task_completion.description: Draw the outer L-shaped building perimeter
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace canvas shows a closed loop of 6 line segments forming an L-shape.

task_completion.description: Anchor the outer perimeter to the origin (0,0)
task_completion.visual_evidence: One of the vertices of the outer L-shape (typically the bottom-left corner) is constrained to the origin, indicated by a coincident constraint icon at the intersection of the X and Y axes.

task_completion.description: Apply the specified dimensions to the outer perimeter edges
task_completion.visual_evidence: Dimension labels are visible on the outer edges: 6000 (left vertical), 3000 (top horizontal), 2000 (inner vertical), 2000 (inner horizontal), 4000 (right vertical), and 5000 (bottom horizontal).
A second closed L-shaped loop of 6 line segments is drawn inside the outer perimeter.

task_completion.description: Apply a uniform 150mm wall thickness constraint
task_completion.visual_evidence: Distance constraints of '150' are visible between the parallel line segments of the inner and outer perimeters, establishing the uniform wall thickness.

task_completion.description: Ensure the sketch is fully constrained
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace property browser/status area (usually top left) displays 'ok' or '0 DOF' (degrees of freedom), indicating the sketch is fully constrained.

task_completion.description: Save the sketch to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace window title bar displays 'l_shape_floor_plan.slvs', or a save dialog is visible showing this filename being saved in the '~/Documents/SolveSpace/' directory.

integrity.description: Agent used the SolveSpace GUI to create the floor plan
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with the SolveSpace graphical interface (drawing tools, constraint menus), not a text editor modifying an .slvs XML/text file.
The dimensions (6000, 3000, 150, etc.) appear as actual SolveSpace constraint labels (usually purple/magenta text with lines), not just text annotations or manually dragged unconstrained lines.

integrity.description: Agent saved the file through the application
integrity.visual_evidence: The file is saved using SolveSpace's 'File -> Save' or 'Save As' functionality, rather than being created via terminal commands like 'touch', 'echo', or 'curl'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path is /home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/l_shape_floor_plan.slvs. The wall thickness must be exactly 150mm. The outer vertices should correspond to [[0, 0], [0, 6000], [3000, 6000], [3000, 4000], [5000, 4000], [5000, 0]] and inner vertices to [[150, 150], [150, 5850], [2850, 5850], [2850, 3850], [4850, 3850], [4850, 150]]. This geometry results from the 150mm offset and the specified edge lengths: 6000 (left), 3000 (top), 2000 (inner vertical), 2000 (inner horizontal), 4000 (right), 5000 (bottom).
